name: py-tp-hallucinated-call
desc: bare call to a nonexistent symbol must be flagged

name: py-tp-typo-with-suggestion
desc: one-edit typo of a known symbol is flagged with a did-you-mean

name: py-tn-known-symbol
desc: calling an IR-known symbol is clean

name: py-tn-builtins-and-exceptions
desc: print/len/isinstance and exception constructors never flag

name: py-tn-private-helper-from-infile
desc: private defs in the on-disk file are known via in-file context

name: py-tn-from-import-fold
desc: names bound by from-imports in the on-disk file are known

name: py-tn-multiline-import-paren
desc: parenthesized multi-line from-import binds all names

name: py-tn-import-binding-call
desc: a from-imported class used as a constructor is known

name: py-tn-docstring-and-comment
desc: docstring prose and comments naming calls must not flag
edit:
def cleanup_task():
    """Runs cleanup_all() and reports COUNT(errors)."""
    # TODO: wire process_all() here
    pass

name: py-tn-multiline-sql-string
desc: triple-quoted SQL spanning consecutive lines stays blanked
edit:
QUERY = """
SELECT COUNT(*), MAX(created_at)
FROM events
"""

name: py-tn-same-edit-definition
desc: a function defined in the same edit may be called in the same edit

name: py-tp-fstring-interpolation-call
desc: FIX: a hallucinated call inside an f-string {...} interpolation must be flagged (was a silent FN — the whole f-string interior was blanked)

name: py-tn-fstring-known-call
desc: FIX: a known symbol called inside an f-string interpolation is clean (interpolation scanned, not flagged)

name: py-tn-fstring-prose-and-escapes
desc: FIX: literal text and {{ }} brace escapes in an f-string must NOT read as calls (only the real interpolation is scanned)
edit:
def msg(n):
    return f"{{not_a_call()}} count is COUNT() = {n}"

name: py-fp-local-callable
desc: KNOWN FP: a local variable holding a callable cannot be resolved statically

name: py-async-def
desc: `async def name(` is recognized as a definition, so the self-reference is not flagged
edit:
async def fetch_all(urls):
    return [u for u in urls]
